Its anchors are real and growing: the medical education cluster, the international cricket stadium at Mullanpur, and direct proximity to Chandigarh's northern sectors — the most prestigious address in the region."}],"$L4"]]}],"$L5","$L6"],"$L7","$L8"]}]}],"$L9"]}],["$La"],"$Lb"]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"CiuaWHgJFbzipCGejtyC4"}
4:["$","p","1",{"style":{"color":"rgba(var(--fg-rgb),0.55)","fontSize":"16px","lineHeight":1.95,"marginBottom":"20px"},"children":"The thesis here is patience. Infrastructure is being built ahead of population, which means the buyer is paying for tomorrow's neighbourhood today. For buyers with a five-to-ten-year horizon who want large, clean plots in the Tricity's most planned environment, New Chandigarh remains the strongest structural story."}]
5:["$","div","2",{"style":{"marginBottom":"48px"},"children":[["$","h2",null,{"style":{"color":"var(--fg)","fontSize":"clamp(20px, 2.5vw, 26px)","fontWeight":600,"letterSpacing":"-0.5px","lineHeight":1.3,"marginBottom":"24px"},"children":[["$","span",null,{"style":{"color":"var(--accent)","marginRight":"12px"},"children":"◈"}],"The case for Mohali"]}],[["$","p","0",{"style":{"color":"rgba(var(--fg-rgb),0.55)","fontSize":"16px","lineHeight":1.95,"marginBottom":"20px"},"children":"Mohali is the opposite trade: a functioning city with employment, retail, healthcare, an international airport, and a deep rental market. Sectors along the IT City corridor, Aerocity, and the JLPL belt offer something New Chandigarh cannot yet — income and immediate usability. A commercial showroom on an arterial Mohali road earns rent from year one; a residential plot can be built on and occupied immediately."}],["$","p","1",{"style":{"color":"rgba(var(--fg-rgb),0.55)","fontSize":"16px","lineHeight":1.95,"marginBottom":"20px"},"children":"Appreciation in Mohali is more sector-specific — driven by road frontage, corridor position, and commercial activity — which is exactly why local, plot-level knowledge matters more here than anywhere else in the Tricity."}]]]}]
6:["$","div","3",{"style":{"marginBottom":"48px"},"children":[["$","h2",null,{"style":{"color":"var(--fg)","fontSize":"clamp(20px, 2.5vw, 26px)","fontWeight":600,"letterSpacing":"-0.5px","lineHeight":1.3,"marginBottom":"24px"},"children":[["$","span",null,{"style":{"color":"var(--accent)","marginRight":"12px"},"children":"◈"}],"Our framework"]}],[["$","p","0",{"style":{"color":"rgba(var(--fg-rgb),0.55)","fontSize":"16px","lineHeight":1.95,"marginBottom":"20px"},"children":"If your goal is long-horizon capital growth with no income requirement, New Chandigarh deserves the larger share. If you want usability, rental yield, or a commercial asset, Mohali is the market. Many of our clients hold both — a growth plot in New Chandigarh and an income asset in Mohali — and treat the pair as one balanced Tricity position."}],["$","p","1",{"style":{"color":"rgba(var(--fg-rgb),0.55)","fontSize":"16px","lineHeight":1.95,"marginBottom":"20px"},"children":"As always: this is general commentary, not personal investment advice.
